topic: A college student discovers their roommate has been reading their journal
raw_output:
[story 1]

The scent of old paper and stale coffee suddenly felt suffocating. I kept glancing over my shoulder, a nervous twitch in my jaw. My journal, usually a sanctuary tucked under my pillow, was gone. I found it later, open on the desk, the pages filled with my most private anxieties, underlined with a faint, unfamiliar ink. My heart hammered against my ribs, a frantic drum solo in the quiet dorm room. Every creak of the floorboards sounded like a footstep. I started locking my door, triple-checking the deadbolt, even though I knew it was pointless. The air in the room felt thick, charged with unspoken scrutiny.

[story 2]

It started subtly—a misplaced bookmark, a page turned slightly differently than I’d left it. Now, the realization had curdled into a cold, tight knot in my stomach. I watched my roommate across the room, their attention fixed on the open book resting on my nightstand. I couldn't look away. I picked up my journal, my fingers trembling so badly I almost dropped it. When I flipped to a specific entry, the handwriting seemed… different. It wasn't mine. A wave of intense unease washed over me, making my palms sweat. I began counting the seconds, waiting for some inevitable, unwelcome confirmation.

[story 3]

The silence in the room was deafening, amplifying the frantic rhythm of my own breathing. I kept moving, pacing the small space, my gaze darting from the window to the door, then back to the desk. When I finally approached, the sight of my journal lying open, the words I’d only shared with the universe now laid bare before my roommate, sent a jolt of icy dread through me. My voice came out thin, barely a whisper. "What are you doing with this?" The response was too calm, too neutral, and it only tightened the knot of apprehension in my chest. I stood frozen, waiting for the inevitable shift in their expression, the sign that this was not just a misunderstanding.
